summary: I was born in 2001 at Ban Don Yang Refugee Camp in Thailand. My family fled from Burma and lived there on the border, seeking safety from the Burmese government. Life in the camp was peaceful but full of hardship, with little money and limited education. It felt like a black hole at night, and we had no real hope for the future. Eventually, we decided to leave the camp to start a new life in America where opportunities were better.

Thailand was not my home, but I lived there in the refugee camp near the border. After fleeing Burma, my family stayed in Thailand where we received new clothes and stayed in a hotel before flying to the United States. Although it was a tiny community and life was hard, I remember feeling fresh and hopeful when we prepared to leave. The journey through Thailand was difficult, but it was a necessary step before starting our new life abroad.

My family originally came from Burma, but we had to flee because we feared the government there. We left everything behind to find safety and peace. I was very young and don’t remember much about what I left, but I do remember the last time I played with my friends before we escaped. Burma was the place we called home, but it was no longer safe for us to stay.

My family and I moved to Saint Paul, Minnesota after leaving the refugee camp and Thailand. Starting life there was hard because of the cold weather and the language barrier, but I enjoyed going to school and getting used to the city’s bright streets and wide roads. Over the years, I attended four different schools and slowly adapted to my new environment. Saint Paul became my new home where I found more food, money, and hope for the future.

The United States represented hope and happiness for me and my family. After living in the refugee camp and Thailand, we came here so the children could get a better education and we could have a better life. Although it was difficult at first, especially with the language and new culture, I grew to enjoy living here. The U.S. gave me opportunities I never had before, and I hope to find success in the future.
